persona.the_one_thing: She is Anthropic's working-level 'Applied AI Architect' for the Korea developer ecosystem — not an exec — so walk in with a concrete, buildable Claude use-case, not a partnership pitch (high confidence; based on her title and recurring hackathon/symposium appearances, s54/s55/s57).
persona.snapshot: Jooneun Choi is an Applied AI Architect at Anthropic — the hands-on technical role that bridges Claude's capabilities to real-world deployments. She is active in Anthropic's Korea developer push: a panelist on the 'Multimodal AI' track at the Global AI Frontiers Symposium 2026 and part of Anthropic's team for the 'Push to Prod' Anthropic×Replit hackathon series in Seoul and a Daejeon startup event. This sits against the backdrop of Anthropic opening its Seoul office on June 17, 2026.
persona.whats_new: Anthropic opened its Seoul office (June 17, 2026, its third in APAC after Tokyo and Bengaluru); Jooneun is appearing as the technical/applied face at Korea AI events — the AI Frontiers Symposium 2026 multimodal panel and the Anthropic×Replit 'Push to Prod' hackathons.

recent_activity.what: Panelist, Global AI Frontiers Symposium 2026 — Track B, Multimodal AI (titled 'Applied AI Architect, Anthropic')
Listed on Anthropic's team for 'Push to Prod SEOUL' hackathon (Anthropic × Replit × Korea Investment)
Listed among Anthropic participants for a Daejeon startup event co-hosted with Replit

top_moves.fact: Titled 'Applied AI Architect, Anthropic' at the AI Frontiers Symposium 2026
top_moves.inference: Her job is to judge real implementation feasibility on Claude, not to sign partnership headlines (that's the Representative Director level).
top_moves.move: Come with one concrete Orbt use-case and the technical specifics of how it would run on Claude — let her evaluate something buildable, not a vision deck.

top_moves.fact: On Anthropic's team for the Anthropic×Replit 'Push to Prod' hackathons in Seoul and Daejeon
top_moves.inference: She works in and around fast prototype-to-production developer culture ('push to prod'), so she rewards things that can ship quickly.
top_moves.move: Frame Orbt as something you could prototype on Claude in days; show or describe what a working v1 looks like rather than a roadmap.

top_moves.fact: Seated on the symposium's 'Multimodal AI' track
top_moves.inference: Multimodal is a topic she engages with publicly and is comfortable going deep on.
top_moves.move: If Orbt touches multimodal at all, lead with it; otherwise ask her view on multimodal applications in Korea to open a real technical exchange.

non_obvious.insight: Anthropic's Korea story is told publicly through KiYoung Choi, the Representative Director/CEO (a different person, no relation implied) — but the recurring Anthropic name at the actual builder events (the Seoul and Daejeon hackathons plus the multimodal symposium) is Jooneun Choi. She reads as the de-facto applied/technical point person for Korea's developer ecosystem, even though the press centers the exec hire.

honest_gaps: No first-person/authored content was found for Jooneun Choi — no LinkedIn posts, quotes, or interviews confirmed as authored by the target; all sources are about/third-party listings.
Location, tenure at Anthropic, prior career history, and exact seniority are unconfirmed.

The handle 'jooneun' from the seed LinkedIn URL was not tied to a verified profile in the sources (several same-name 'Joon/Jooeun/Jooneun' profiles in the source set are different people and were excluded).
MBTI omitted by design: no grounded signal exists for any letter without first-person data.
Confirm live: her actual scope (developer relations vs. enterprise solutions), whether multimodal is her core area or one panel topic, and what use-cases she's currently prioritizing in Korea.
